item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCp wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as there's no indication either image has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of harvesters left to rot is really interesting, but the expungements hurt the scip real bad. Downvoted.

I also corrected some missing words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The other issue with it apparently being in the UK is that it's mentioned that police shot at the car - police officers in the UK are generally not armed with guns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somewhat relevant to this article and perhaps a demonstration of convergent evolution of concepts, I just learned of an old Czechoslovak urban legend of the black ambulance car, supposedly riding around cities at night, harvesting young people for organs to sell to rich people in the imperialist West.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you know who controls these taxis? SCP-1281! Get it? Get it? …Never mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Taxi of death! Don't get in, the price it too high!

Murder cars! I can dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, although it hasn't occured to me. I was discussing in chat, and Voct suggested the number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume the number you chose for this SCP was intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha, of course SCP-1729 would be about taxis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the second…. well, an alteration thereof. Might even be bothered to fix it at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cartoon Network show called Regular Show. One episode has them watching a corny b-movie where a black cab taxi comes to life and hunts people down to eat them while yelling "'ello guvnah!" at them.

As for the gun thing, you could simply swap MI5 for FBI and have it in America, or if you're dead set on it being in the UK… I dunno. Have it accidentally drive near a hostage situation and get shot? Have it ram the two cops out of the way to escape, they get in their car and give chase, and it ends up crashing into a tree?

My brain is not good at doing writing tonight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What episode of what?

Also, you got me pants down there.. I made the classic mistake of spotting the problem , having no idea how to fix it, and hoping people won't notice, since I def. didn't when originally writing it.
Yeah, I fail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"When approached by the summoned patrol, the vehicle activated and attempted to ride away, injuring one of the officers in the process - the second responded with gunfire, causing it to crash due to tire damage."

Police in the UK aren't armed, and an armed response team is not going to be summoned to a "car smells odd" situation. I'm assuming they're UK police because of the MI5 (a british organisation) reference. "Patrolmen" generally isn't used to describe UK police, either. I think the term is "beat officer" but honestly I'm stumbling in the dark there.

Other than that I quite like it, but it keeps making me think of that episode of Regular Show and that makes me unable to upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent. It's weird, but it sticks to the concept and really goes through with it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe
'komunikacie' would be the word that's used in slovak road law - I thought english uses it in both ways too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon activation, instances of SCP-1729 proceed move along ground communications in a random pattern;

I think you mean "ground roadways" or something similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For fuck's sake, how.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nearing the end of the article, I suddenly had an intense, irrational fear that this was going to be another AWCY item. I don't quite know what to make of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe I only saw it post-edit, but I liked it. +1.
…. I suddenly realize I've been saying that a lot lately, hmmm..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are exempt from monthly terminations in this case. Made an edit that should clarify it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zero fucks.

None at all? Not even one?

Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can I make edits, not to content but to wording and tone? Say, change "doesn't" to "does not" and other such corrections to make it seem like a scientific document?

Also, I like the explanation. Now I just wonder what some weird, SCP-making organization wanted human tissue for in large quantities….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edited the end note a bit. Hopefully the point comes across better.

Explanation of sorts, since I'm not sure it gets across as of now
– hide block

These things are automated harvesters indeed. However, by the time we found the first one, they were abandoned by whoever used them.. .except they gave zero fucks, and left them running. So now, they collect people, process them, and deliver to nobody at all, and do it until we capture them , and/or they break down on their own - they have a finite supply of material to be used, after all. The foundation got a hint, and stopped monitoring the delivery locations, hence, last addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of predatory, auto-mated cars but as Smapti said there are major tone issues. Upvoted, but I hope the tone issues are fixed. Can we edit them ourselves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The engine doesn't connect to the gas tank.

There are a few instances like that - to me, using contractions like "doesn't" and "isn't" comes off as too informal and out-of-place in a Foundation document. There are a few punctuation errors aside from that. All in all, the article is well-written enough that the tone problems don't hurt it too badly, but I just don't like the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest, I don't see the similarity, though this does point to me not getting some things across too well.

I wonder though, what tone issues?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know. The idea was that for the lot there, terminations were suspended indefinitely. Maybe it's silly but while writing it, I got a bit cutesy with definitions - DR being Disposable, Retained. Might be better to cut the renumbering out.

EDIT: And now, when I posted a second comment there, the old one was displayed properly. This could relate to what the fuck is causing the bug.
Play it off, admin cat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Smapti, the "horrible murder bit" is a bit cheesy. when i viewed the seond image i thought the SCP ocassionally had limbs and succh parts manifesting on parts of the car as to be slightly malicious, still the syntax is well written so therefore No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, on the other hand, like the implications of these being harvesters for some unknown organization. Perhaps a tale is in order hinting towards who?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not going to downvote it, although I agree with Smapti's comment. Your second image bears a caption that says an instance of 1729 malfunctioned. I like that idea a lot better, with these things being essentially benign, but malfunctioning sometimes.
I'm upvoting for potential though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to upvote this in spite of some tone issues, but then I got to the "and then it kills you horribly" part and changed my mind. We already have a British public transport system with a phantom driver, and this one doesn't really do anything for me as is. Honestly, i'd find it much more interesting if it didn't kill its customers unless they did something like curse at him or try to short him on the fare, and its main effect were something more unusual - such as, for instance, the passenger getting switched places with their counterpart from a parallel universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enghh, I like it. It may be because I'm British and live within a kilometre of a Teso's, but this is good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the concept is good, but i think that get high qualified D class personnel is a little bit excessive. Rememberthe Foundation usually takes D class personnel from prisoners, sentenced to death.

P.S: i like the second picture, thats why i think this can be good

i will vote later (after editing)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehhh, concept does nothing for me, but its quality is enough to stop me from downvoting.

No vote.
